source.notes: Structured from CompTIA Security+ SY0-701 Exam Objectives v4.0.

objectives.title: Security controls
concepts: Control categories: Technical, Managerial, Operational, Physical
Control types: Preventive, Deterrent, Detective, Corrective, Compensating, Directive

objectives.title: Fundamental security concepts
concepts: Physical security: Bollards, Access control vestibule, Fencing, Video surveillance, Security guard
Deception and disruption: Honeypot, Honeynet, Honeyfile, Honeytoken

objectives.title: Change management processes and their importance to security
concepts: Business processes: Approval process, Ownership, Stakeholders, Impact analysis, Test results, Backout plan, Maintenance window, Standard operating procedure
Technical implications: Allow lists/deny lists, Restricted activities, Downtime, Service restart, Legacy applications, Dependencies

objectives.title: Cryptographic solutions
concepts: PKI: Public key, Private key, Key escrow
Encryption: Level (full-disk, partition, file, volume, database, record), Transport/communication, Asymmetric, Symmetric, Key exchange, Algorithms, Key length
Tools: TPM, HSM, Key management system, Secure enclave
Hashing, Salting, Digital signatures, Key stretching
Certificates: Certificate authorities, CRL, OCSP, Self-signed, Third-party, Root of trust, CSR, Wildcard

objectives.title: Threat actors and motivations
concepts: Threat actors: Nation-state, Unskilled attacker, Hacktivist, Insider threat, Organized crime, Shadow IT
Motivations: Data exfiltration, Espionage, Service disruption, Blackmail, Financial gain, Philosophical/political, Ethical, Revenge, Disruption/chaos, War

objectives.title: Threat vectors and attack surfaces
concepts: Supply chain: Managed service providers, Vendor, Supplier
Human vectors: Phishing, Vishing, Smishing, Misinformation/disinformation, Impersonation, Business email compromise, Pretexting, Watering hole, Brand impersonation, Typosquatting

objectives.title: Types of vulnerabilities
concepts: Application: Memory injection, Buffer overflow, Race conditions, Malicious update

objectives.title: Indicators of malicious activity
concepts: Malware attacks: Ransomware, Trojan, Worm, Spyware, Bloatware, Virus, Keylogger, Logic bomb, Rootkit
Physical attacks: Brute force, RFID cloning, Environmental
Network attacks: DDoS, DNS attacks, Wireless, On-path, Credential replay, Malicious code
Application attacks: Injection, Buffer overflow, Replay, Privilege escalation, Forgery, Directory traversal
Indicators: Account lockout, Concurrent session, Blocked content, Impossible travel, Resource consumption, Resource inaccessibility, Out-of-cycle logging, Published/documented attacks, Missing logs

objectives.title: Mitigation techniques
concepts: Hardening techniques: Encryption, Installation of endpoint protection, Host-based firewall, HIDS, Disabling ports/protocols, Default password changes, Removal of unnecessary software

objectives.title: Architecture models and security implications
concepts: Cloud: Responsibility matrix, Hybrid, Third-party vendors, IaaS, PaaS, SaaS
Network infrastructure: Physical isolation (air gap), Logical segmentation, SDN

objectives.title: Security principles for enterprise infrastructure
concepts: Infrastructure considerations: Device placement, Security zones, Attack surface, Connectivity, Failure modes, Device attribute (active/passive, inline/tap/monitor)
Network appliances: Jump server, Proxy server, IPS/IDS, HSM, Sensors, Collectors, Log aggregators, Firewalls
Secure communication/access: VPN, TLS, SD-WAN, SASE

objectives.title: Data protection concepts and strategies
concepts: Data types: Regulated, Trade secret, Intellectual property, Legal information, Financial, Human/non-human readable
Data classifications: Sensitive, Confidential, Public, Restricted, Private, Critical
General data considerations: Data states (at rest, in transit, in use), Data sovereignty, Geolocation
Methods: Geographic restrictions, Encryption, Hashing, Masking, Tokenization, Obfuscation, Segmentation, Permission restrictions

objectives.title: Resilience and recovery in security architecture
concepts: Site considerations: Hot site, Cold site, Warm site, Geographic dispersion
Testing: Tabletop exercises, Failover, Simulation, Parallel processing
Backups: Onsite/offsite, Frequency, Encryption, Snapshots, Recovery, Replication, Journaling
Power: UPS, Generator, Dual supply, PDU

objectives.title: Security techniques for computing resources
concepts: Hardening targets: Mobile devices, Workstations, Switches, Routers, Cloud, Servers, ICS/SCADA, EmbeddedOT
Mobile solutions: MDM, Deployment models (BYOD, COPE, CYOD), Connection methods
Wireless security: WiFi (WPA2/WPA3, TKIP, CCMP/AES, SAE), Bluetooth
Application security: Input validation, Secure cookies, Static/dynamic code analysis, Code signing

objectives.title: Asset management security implications
concepts: Disposal/decommissioning: Sanitization, Destruction, Certification, Data retention

objectives.title: Vulnerability management
concepts: Identification methods: Vulnerability scan, Application security, Threat feed, Penetration testing, Responsible disclosure, Bug bounty, System/process audit
Analysis: Confirmation, Prioritize, CVSS, CVE, Vulnerability classification, Exposure factor, Environmental variables, Industry/organizational impact, Risk tolerance
Vulnerability response: Patching, Insurance, Segmentation, Compensating controls, Exceptions and exemptions

objectives.title: Security alerting and monitoring
concepts: Monitoring computing resources: Systems, Applications, Infrastructure
Activities: Log aggregation, Alerting, Scanning, Reporting, Archiving, Alert response and remediation/validation
Tools: SCAP, Benchmarks, Agents/agentless, SIEM, Antivirus, DLP, SNMP traps, NetFlow, Vulnerability scanners

objectives.title: Modifying enterprise capabilities to enhance security
concepts: Firewall: Rules, Access lists, Ports/protocols, Screened subnets
Web filter: Agent-based, Centralized proxy, URL scanning, Content categorization, Block rules, Reputation
Operating system security: Group Policy, SELinux
Implementation of secure protocols: DNSSEC, SSH, S/MIME, SRTP, LDAPS, FTPS, SFTP, SNMPv3, SSL/TLS, HTTPS, IPSec
Email security: DKIM, DMARC, SPF, Gateway

objectives.title: Identity and access management
concepts: Access controls: Mandatory (MAC), Discretionary (DAC), Role-based (RBAC), Rule-based, Attribute-based (ABAC), Time-of-day restrictions, Least privilege
Multifactor authentication: Implementations (biometrics, hardtoken, softtoken, SMS, email, TOTP/HOTP, phone call, push notifications)
Password concepts: Length, Complexity, Reuse, Expiration, Age, Password managers, Passwordless
Privileged access management: Just-in-time, PAM, Ephemeral credentials

objectives.title: Automation and orchestration in security operations
concepts: Use cases: User provisioning, Resource provisioning, Guard rails, Security groups, Ticket creation, Escalation, Enabling/disabling services, Continuous integration/delivery
Benefits: Efficiency, Enforcing baselines, Standard infrastructure configurations, Secure scaling, Employee retention, Reaction time, Workforce multiplier
Other considerations: Complexity, Cost, Single point of failure, Technical debt, Ongoing supportability

objectives.title: Incident response activities
concepts: Process: Preparation, Detection, Analysis, Containment, Eradication, Recovery, Lessons learned
Digital forensics: Legal hold, Chain of custody, Acquisition, Reporting, Preservation, E-discovery

objectives.title: Data sources for investigations
concepts: Log data: Firewall, Application, Endpoint, OS, IPS/IDS, Network, Metadata
Data sources: Vulnerability scans, Automated reports, Dashboards, Packet captures

objectives.title: Elements of effective security governance
concepts: Policies: AUP, Information security, Business continuity, Disaster recovery, Incident response, SDLC, Change management
Standards: Password, Access control, Physical security, Encryption
External considerations: Regulatory, Legal, Industry, Local/regional, National, Global
Types of governance structures: Boards, Committees, Government entities, Centralized/decentralized
Roles and responsibilities: Owners, Controllers, Processors, Custodians/stewards

objectives.title: Risk management
concepts: Risk assessment: Ad hoc, Recurring, One-time, Continuous
Risk analysis: Qualitative, Quantitative, Single loss expectancy (SLE), Annualized loss expectancy (ALE), Annualized rate of occurrence (ARO), Probability, Likelihood, Exposure factor, Impact
Risk register: Key risk indicators, Risk owners, Risk threshold
Risk management strategies: Transfer, Accept (exemption/exception), Avoid, Mitigate
Business impact analysis: RTO, RPO, MTTR, MTBF

objectives.title: Third-party risk assessment and management
concepts: Vendor assessment: Penetration testing, Right-to-audit clause, Evidence of internal audits, Independent assessments, Supply chain analysis
Vendor selection: Due diligence, Conflict of interest
Agreement types: SLA, MOA, MOU, MSA, Work order/SOW, NDA, BPA

objectives.title: Security compliance
concepts: Consequences of non-compliance: Fines, Sanctions, Reputational damage, Loss of license, Contractual impacts
Compliance monitoring: Due diligence, Due care, Attestation, Acknowledgement, Internal/external
Privacy: Legal implications (local/regional/national/global), Data subject, Controller vs processor, Ownership, Data inventory and retention, Right to be forgotten

objectives.title: Audits and assessments
concepts: External: Regulatory, Examinations, Assessment, Independent third-party audit
Penetration testing: Physical, Offensive, Defensive, Integrated, Known/unknown/partially known environment, Reconnaissance, Pivot, Active/passive

objectives.title: Security awareness practices
concepts: Anomalous behavior recognition: Risky, Unexpected, Unintentional

User guidance and training: Policy/handbooks, Situational awareness, Insider threat, Password management, Removable media and cables, Social engineering, Operational security, Hybrid/remote work environments
